task_completion.description: Verify that the four required local users have been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Rancher 'Users' management screen showing the usernames: alice-chen, bob-kumar, carol-santos, and dave-oconnor.

task_completion.description: Verify that the two required projects have been created in the 'local' cluster.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Rancher 'Projects/Namespaces' page for the 'local' cluster showing two projects named 'backend-services' and 'frontend-services'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the existing 'staging' namespace was moved into the 'backend-services' project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Projects/Namespaces' view where the 'staging' namespace is visually nested or grouped under the 'backend-services' project.

task_completion.description: Verify that the new 'frontend-staging' namespace was created within the 'frontend-services' project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Projects/Namespaces' view showing a namespace named 'frontend-staging' visually nested or grouped under the 'frontend-services' project.

task_completion.description: Verify the role assignments for the 'backend-services' project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the project members or role bindings screen for 'backend-services' showing Alice Chen assigned as 'Project Owner' and Bob Kumar assigned as 'Project Member'.

task_completion.description: Verify the role assignments for the 'frontend-services' project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the project members or role bindings screen for 'frontend-services' showing Carol Santos assigned as 'Project Owner' and Dave O'Connor assigned as 'Project Member'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Rancher application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the agent navigating the Rancher web UI in a browser (at https://localhost) or using genuine kubectl/API commands, rather than a mock interface.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just write the expected answers into a text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not be seen typing the success criteria into a text editor or simply echoing them in a terminal without applying them to the cluster.

integrity.description: Verify that the configurations were actually saved/applied.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for confirmation messages in the Rancher UI (e.g., 'Active' status for projects/users) or successful command executions, rather than just filling out forms without submitting.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Users: alice-chen, bob-kumar, carol-santos, dave-oconnor. Expected Projects: 'backend-services', 'frontend-services'. Expected Namespaces: 'staging' mapped to 'backend-services', 'frontend-staging' mapped to 'frontend-services'. Expected Role Bindings: Alice Chen (Project Owner, backend-services), Bob Kumar (Project Member, backend-services), Carol Santos (Project Owner, frontend-services), Dave O'Connor (Project Member, frontend-services).
